event: 37 years old
female
admitted
body mass index of 67 kg/m2
back pain
gastroesophageal reflux disease
obstructive sleep apnea
hyperlipidemia
hypertension
tried to lose weight without success
underwent a Da Vinci-assisted, laparoscopic, one-stage, single-anastomosis DS procedure
terminal ileum was identified
ran 250 cm proximally up the ileum
defined the length of the common channel
marked the site of our duodeno-ileal anastomosis
greater omentum was taken from the greater curvature
distally 3 cm from the pylorus
a window was created for transection of the duodenum
sleeve gastrectomy was done
loosely over a 34 French bougie-sized Edlich tube
the staple line was oversewn
the duodenum was transected 3 cm from the pylorus
the duodenal stump was oversewn with a running absorbable suture
a two-layer hand sewn anastomosis between the duodenum and the ileum
created at the 250 cm mark
the anastomosis was tested with methylene blue and air
showed no leakage
a drain was placed in left upper quadrant
the stomach was removed
an upper gastrointestinal study (UGI) on postoperative day (POD) 2
was negative for a leak
bariatric phase 1 diet was started
tolerated it well
discharged
developed persistent nausea, fatigue, and severe abdominal pain
came to the emergency department
found to be tachycardic and anemic with a leukocytosis
abdominal exam revealed significant distension with guarding
an UGI was negative
computed tomography (CT) of the abdomen showing free fluid
suggestive of a leak or bleeding
taken to the operating room (OR) for diagnostic laparoscopy
found to have hemoperitoneum
evacuated
active bleeding source was not identifiable
complete inspection of the gastroileostomy anastomosis
was performed without significant findings
there was no leakage seen with a methylene blue test
an attempt to explore the duodenal stump
was made but was unsuccessful due to retraction of the duodenal stump
and inflammation around the tissue
Three large 19F Blake drains were placed
transfused with 2 units of packed red blood cells (PRBCs)
intra-operatively
kept intubated and sent to intensive care unit (ICU)
for further monitoring
became hypotensive, unresponsive to fluids
and her abdominal drain had increased sanguinous output
her second drain showed frank bile
became tachycardic
taken back to the OR for exploratory laparotomy
with the presumptive diagnosis of bleeding with duodenal stump blowout
underwent laparotomy through a generous right subcostal incision
encountered a large amount of hemoperitoneum
temporized with packing identified as coming from the gastroduodenal artery
which was over-sewn
found to have total stump blow
and the staple line was completely undone
placed a Malecot tube in the duodenum
and over-sewn with a purse-string suture
the abdomen was closed and drains were placed
blood loss was 2500 cc
received 5 units of PRBCs and 4 units of fresh frozen plasma
intra-operatively
transferred to the ICU
post-operative course was complicated by hemorrhagic fluid collections
seen on CT scan and atelectasis
developed acute thrombosis of the left internal jugular
and subclavian veins at a previous central catheter site
without pulmonary embolism
placed on therapeutic Lovenox and octreotide
given intravenous piperacillin/taxobactam
which was later changed to cefazolin
successfully discharged on POD 17
to her home with home health care
for dressing changes and total parenteral nutrition
